etymology.description: 秋(秋) (qiū, "autumn") + 天(天) (tiān, "sky; heaven; weather") combine to mean "autumn". In this context, the characters suggest the 'autumn season', linking the time of year (秋) with the general concept of time or weather (天).
etymology.interpretation: 秋(秋) (qiū, "autumn") represents the season following summer, characterized by harvest time. 天(天) (tiān, "sky; heaven; weather") commonly denotes weather or a period in general. The term 秋天 expresses the particular segment of time within a year designated as autumn, combining the notion of this transitional period with its atmospheric and temporal nuances that 天 implies.

examples.chinese: 秋天是一个收获的季节(秋天是一個收穫的季節)。
examples.pinyin: Qiūtiān shì yī ge shōuhuò de jìjié.
examples.english: Autumn is a season of harvest.

examples.chinese: 秋天到了(秋天到了)，树叶变成了金黄色(樹葉變成了金黃色)。
examples.pinyin: Qiūtiān dào le, shùyè biàn chéng le jīnhuángsè.
examples.english: Autumn has arrived, and the leaves have turned golden yellow.

examples.chinese: 在秋天(在秋天)，我们常去公园散步(我們常去公園散步)。
examples.pinyin: Zài qiūtiān, wǒmen cháng qù gōngyuán sànbù.
examples.english: In autumn, we often go for walks in the park.
